title: palmwtc: Automated whole-tree chamber workflow for oil-palm ecophysiology
description: palmwtc processes data from automated whole-tree chambers (WTC) sized to enclose individual oil palm trees, instrumented with LI-COR LI-850 gas analyzers. It performs multi-stage quality control, computes CO2 and H2O fluxes from concentration cycles, validates against literature ecophysiology references, and produces inputs for the XPalm digital-twin calibration pipeline. To our knowledge, this is the first whole-tree-chamber deployment for oil palm.
references: Medlyn, B. E., Zaehle, S., De Kauwe, M. G. (2016). Using models to guide field experiments: a priori predictions for the CO2 response of a nutrient- and water-limited native Eucalypt woodland. Global Change Biology, 22(8), 2834-2851. doi:10.1111/gcb.13268
